sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 219, 221, 222 & 223 of 2018 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection Nos.6,19 & 20 2. For hearing of main case 08.12.2025 Mr. Faheem Ali Memon advocate holds brief for Dr. Shahnawaz, advocate for the applicant These reference applications are pending since 2018 without any progress and remains listed in fresh cases. On 21.10.2020 and 17.02.2022 time was sought. On the last date applicant remained unrepresented without intimation and / or justification. Even today more than seven years later matters remain listed for orders on office objection and once again brief is being held for applicant’s counsel and adjournment is being sought for no cogent reason whatsoever. It appears that the applicant is not interested in proceeding with the matters. Accordingly, in view of judgment of the Supreme Court in the case of C.I.R. vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, these reference applications are dismissed for non- prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in connected matters. Judge Judge Amjad
